name: Heap Analytics API
what_it_does: Provides server-side event tracking and data export APIs for Heap's product analytics platform, which autocaptures all user interactions by default and allows agents to push server-side events and export behavioral data.
use_cases: Send server-side events (e.g., payment completed, subscription upgraded) to Heap to correlate back-end business events with front-end behavior
Add user properties (plan type, cohort, region) to Heap user profiles to enable behavioral segmentation in dashboards
Export raw event data via the Export API for downstream processing in a data warehouse
Identify and merge anonymous and logged-in user identities after a sign-up event
Delete user data on GDPR/CCPA request by programmatically removing a user's event history
not_for: Querying live behavioral data or building dashboards — Heap's query layer is UI-only; the REST API is primarily an ingestion and export interface
Real-time streaming event consumption — the Export API is batch-oriented, not a live stream
Mobile-native event tracking without the Heap mobile SDK — server-side REST does not replace SDK autocapture on native apps
best_when: An agent needs to enrich Heap user profiles with server-side business events that the JavaScript SDK cannot capture, or must export raw session data for ETL pipelines.
avoid_when: The goal is to query or visualize behavioral data programmatically — Heap does not expose a query API; use a connected data warehouse instead.

auth.notes: Server-side track and identify calls use the Heap App ID (not a secret). Export API uses a separate API key. The App ID is considered public (embedded in client-side code); the export key should be kept secret.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan available with limited sessions per month; Export API and server-side API typically require paid Growth or Enterprise plans.
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth and Enterprise plans; pricing not publicly listed, requires sales engagement for larger volumes.
pricing.notes: Free tier is primarily for evaluation. Server-side REST API and Export API are generally growth/enterprise features. Contact sales for pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Track API: up to 25 requests per second per app. Export API: batch exports can take minutes to hours depending on data volume.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Track API returns 200 OK even for malformed events in some cases, making silent data loss a risk. Export API errors are more descriptive but documentation is sparse.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Track calls are not idempotent — duplicate calls create duplicate events. Agents must deduplicate at the source. Identity calls are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: The Track API returns HTTP 200 for many invalid payloads — agents cannot rely on HTTP status to confirm event was recorded correctly
Server-side events do not participate in session recordings or autocaptured click sequences — they appear as standalone events only
User identity must be established before associating server events to a user; events sent before identify() are attributed to an anonymous identity
Export API jobs are asynchronous and can take significant time for large date ranges — agents must poll for completion rather than expecting synchronous responses
Heap App IDs are environment-specific (development vs production) — mixing them silently corrupts production analytics data

security_score_components.security_notes: App ID is intentionally public (embedded in JS). Export API key must be protected as a server-side secret. No fine-grained API key scopes available.
